description_long: This is a compact, vintage-style keyboard with a rectangular form factor and a set of white, slightly yellowed keycaps arranged in a dense grid. The keys are labeled with English characters and symbols, and the keyboard lacks a separate numeric keypad, suggesting a tenkeyless or compact layout. The base of the keyboard is exposed, revealing a brownish-orange circuit board and some visible electronic components along the top edge. The keycaps show signs of wear and discoloration, particularly on the spacebar, indicating frequent use. There are no visible branding marks or logos, and no attached cable or wireless receiver is present. The overall construction appears to be primarily plastic with some metal and electronic components.
description: A compact, vintage keyboard with white, worn keycaps on a visible circuit board, lacking branding or a cable.
description_view_0: Angled view showing the staggered rows of white keycaps and the exposed brown circuit board beneath.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the full key layout, worn spacebar, and absence of a numeric keypad.
description_view_2: Opposite angled view revealing the right edge, green electronic components, and the compact form factor.
description_view_3: Side profile showing the low height and uniform row of keycaps, with the circuit board visible underneath.
